category_title: Students expressing school-related frustration through anger and peer-interaction difficulties
minimal_context: Patrick is assigned to work on a classroom activity with a group of peers.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Patrick, it is time to join the members of the group for our classroom activity.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Kicks the furniture in frustration) I don't want to. I only want to play with my one regular friend.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: He is in another group today. Please sit with these classmates.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: No! I don't want to be friends with them!

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just try to work together with them for this activity.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: You guys are too slow and you're doing it all wrong! (Vocalizes his frustration and impatience with the members of the group, leaving his classmates confused and startled)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Patrick, it is time to join the members of the group for our classroom activity.

STUDENT: (Kicks the furniture in frustration) I don't want to. I only want to play with my one regular friend.

TUTOR: He is in another group today. Please sit with these classmates.

STUDENT: No! I don't want to be friends with them!

TUTOR: Just try to work together with them for this activity.

STUDENT: You guys are too slow and you're doing it all wrong! (Vocalizes his frustration and impatience with the members of the group, leaving his classmates confused and startled)

task.content: Group activities and social interactions with peers in the classroom

challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: vocalized his frustration and impatience with the members of the group, leave classmates confused and startled

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: had ongoing difficulties making and keeping friends
